summary: Probed remote-output capture stream selection on the reference Linux host. The node-scoped Hyprland output and workspace binding worked, portal ScreenCast session setup reached a request path but `SelectSources` failed with Access denied without an owning request-response client, and a direct `grim -o` named-output capture succeeded when run with the live Wayland socket. The node does not unblock `m2-capture-one-frame` because no PipeWire video stream or DMA-BUF format/modifier/timestamp/sync metadata was obtained.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: A node-scoped madobe remote output is created on Linux hardware.
A user-consented portal ScreenCast session is attempted and its PipeWire stream metadata is captured.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: portal-create-session.txt shows a request path, portal-select-sources.err records Access denied, and pipewire-focused-after-portal-error-summary.json shows no video/portal/madobe stream node.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence proves whether the stream maps to the madobe-owned output, including redacted Hyprland, portal, and PipeWire state.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: No PipeWire video stream was produced, so no mapping to the madobe output can be proven. Redacted Hyprland and PipeWire state are present under evidence/m2-capture-stream-proof/.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: If portal mapping cannot be proven, direct named-output capture options and exact blockers are documented.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: direct-grim-named-output-with-wayland-metadata.txt proves direct `grim -o` can target the named output; blocker.md documents why this is insufficient for DMA-BUF frame metadata.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-capture-stream-proof and reports/qd/m2-capture-stream-proof.
m2-capture-one-frame is unblocked only if format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata can be obtained or directly derived from the proven stream path.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: validation-summary.json and blocker.md state that m2-capture-one-frame remains blocked because the direct PNG path does not provide the required metadata.

commandsRun.command: pw-dump focused summary before and after portal attempt
grim -o madobe-qd-m2-capture-stream-proof with explicit XDG_RUNTIME_DIR and WAYLAND_DISPLAY

realWorldValidation.evidence: Live Linux Hyprland/PipeWire/XDPH probing was performed. Direct named-output PNG capture worked, but the portal stream remains blocked until an owning client completes user consent and captures returned stream metadata.

residualRisks: A real portal client may still be able to select the madobe-owned output manually, but this was not proven by the non-interactive bus probe.
The direct `grim` path proves targetability by output name but may not map to the eventual low-latency capture architecture.
